question:
An ideal Rankine cycle uses water as the working fluid. The condenser pressure is 16.0 kPa and the boiler operates at 3.0 MPa. Steam enters the turbine at 577.0°C. The mass flow rate is 79.2 kg/s. Both the pump and turbine are isentropic.

Determine:
(a) The specific enthalpy at each state point (h₁, h₂, h₃, h₄)
(b) The pump work, heat input, and turbine work per unit mass
(c) The net work, thermal efficiency, and net power output

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg
h₄ = ___ kJ/kg
w_pump = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
w_turb = ___ kJ/kg
w_net = ___ kJ/kg
η_th = ___
Ẇ_net = ___ kW
raw_response:
Using the ideal Rankine cycle relations and standard steam-table interpolation:

- State 1: saturated liquid at \(P_1=16.0\ \text{kPa}\)
- State 2: after isentropic pump to \(P_2=3.0\ \text{MPa}\)
- State 3: superheated steam at \(P_3=3.0\ \text{MPa},\ T_3=577.0^\circ\text{C}\)
- State 4: after isentropic turbine expansion to \(P_4=16.0\ \text{kPa}\), so \(s_4=s_3\)

Key relations used:
\[
w_{pump}=v_f(P_2-P_1),\qquad h_2=h_1+w_{pump}
\]
\[
w_{turb}=h_3-h_4,\qquad q_{in}=h_3-h_2
\]
\[
w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{pump},\qquad \eta_{th}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}}
\]
\[
\dot W_{net}=\dot m\, w_{net}
\]

At 16 kPa, saturated-liquid properties give approximately:
\[
h_1 \approx 231.4\ \text{kJ/kg},\quad v_f \approx 0.001016\ \text{m}^3/\text{kg}
\]
So,
\[
w_{pump}\approx 0.001016(3000-16)=3.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
h_2\approx 234.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

At 3.0 MPa and 577°C (superheated steam tables):
\[
h_3 \approx 3617.6\ \text{kJ/kg},\quad s_3 \approx s_4
\]

Expanding isentropically to 16 kPa gives a wet state with \(x_4\approx 0.93\), and
\[
h_4 \approx 2442.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Then:
\[
w_{turb}=3617.6-2442.4=1175.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
q_{in}=3617.6-234.4=3383.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
w_{net}=1175.2-3.0=1172.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
\eta_{th}=1172.2/3383.2=0.346
\]
\[
\dot W_{net}=79.2(1172.2)=92838\ \text{kW}
\]

h₁ = 231.4 kJ/kg  
h₂ = 234.4 kJ/kg  
h₃ = 3617.6 kJ/kg  
h₄ = 2442.4 kJ/kg  
w_pump = 3.0 kJ/kg  
q_in = 3383.2 kJ/kg  
w_turb = 1175.2 kJ/kg  
w_net = 1172.2 kJ/kg  
η_th = 0.346  
Ẇ_net = 92838 kW

question:
An ideal Rankine cycle uses water as the working fluid. The condenser pressure is 20.0 kPa and the boiler operates at 7.5 MPa with a turbine inlet temperature of 572.0°C. The mass flow rate is 16.4 kg/s. Both the pump and turbine are isentropic. Heat is supplied by a source at 1003.2 K and rejected to a sink at 327.6 K. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C (298.15 K), P₀ = 0.1 MPa.

Determine:
(a) All state enthalpies, entropies, and specific flow exergies
(b) Component works, heat transfers, thermal efficiency, and net power
(c) Entropy generation and exergy destruction in each component
(d) Total exergy destruction and second-law efficiency

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg
h₄ = ___ kJ/kg
w_pump = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
w_turb = ___ kJ/kg
w_net = ___ kJ/kg
η_th = ___
Ẇ_net = ___ kW
s₁ = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s₂ = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s₃ = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s₄ = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,pump = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,boiler = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,turb = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,cond = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,total = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
ef₁ = ___ kJ/kg
ef₂ = ___ kJ/kg
ef₃ = ___ kJ/kg
ef₄ = ___ kJ/kg
x_dest,boiler = ___ kJ/kg
x_dest,cond = ___ kJ/kg
x_dest,total = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using steam-table interpolation for water:

- State 1: saturated liquid at 20.0 kPa
- State 2: isentropic pump to 7.5 MPa, with \(w_{pump}\approx v_f(P_2-P_1)\)
- State 3: superheated vapor at 7.5 MPa, 572.0°C
- State 4: isentropic turbine expansion to 20.0 kPa, so \(s_4=s_3\)

Key relations used:
- \(w_{pump}=h_2-h_1\)
- \(q_{in}=h_3-h_2\)
- \(w_{turb}=h_3-h_4\)
- \(w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{pump}\)
- \(\eta_{th}=w_{net}/q_{in}\)
- \(ef=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)\), with \(h_0 \approx 104.86\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_0 \approx 0.3672\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- \(s_{gen,boiler}=(s_3-s_2)-q_{in}/T_H\)
- \(s_{gen,cond}=(s_1-s_4)+q_{out}/T_L\), where \(q_{out}=h_4-h_1\)
- \(x_{dest}=T_0 s_{gen}\)

Below, \(\eta_{II}\) is taken as the exergy efficiency based on source exergy input:
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{w_{net}}{(1-T_0/T_H)\,q_{in}}
\]

h₂ = 259.01 kJ/kg
h₃ = 3578.50 kJ/kg
h₄ = 2291.60 kJ/kg
w_pump = 7.61 kJ/kg
q_in = 3319.49 kJ/kg
w_turb = 1286.90 kJ/kg

η_th = 0.3854

s₂ = 0.8320 kJ/(kg·K)
s₃ = 6.9530 kJ/(kg·K)

s_gen,boiler = 2.8121 kJ/(kg·K)

x_dest,cond = 31.82 kJ/kg
x_dest,total = 870.45 kJ/kg
η_II = 0.5485

If your convention defines second-law efficiency as \(\eta_{th}/\eta_{Carnot}\) with \(T_H=1003.2\ \text{K}\) and \(T_L=327.6\ \text{K}\), then:
\[
\eta_{II,\ Carnot\ basis}\approx 0.5722
\]

question:
An actual Rankine cycle uses water as the working fluid. The condenser pressure is 20.0 kPa and the boiler operates at 8.8 MPa. Steam enters the turbine at 576.0°C. The pump isentropic efficiency is 82.0% and the turbine isentropic efficiency is 88.0%. The mass flow rate is 6.3 kg/s.

Note: η_pump = w_pump,s / w_pump,actual and η_turb = w_turb,actual / w_turb,s.

Determine:
(a) The specific enthalpy at each state point (h₁, h₂s, h₂, h₃, h₄s, h₄)
(b) The pump work, heat input, turbine work, and net work per unit mass
(c) The thermal efficiency and net power output

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂s = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg
h₄s = ___ kJ/kg
h₄ = ___ kJ/kg
w_pump = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
w_turb = ___ kJ/kg
w_net = ___ kJ/kg
η_th = ___
Ẇ_net = ___ kW
raw_response:
Using standard steam-table interpolation:

- At the condenser exit, state 1 is saturated liquid at 20.0 kPa:
  - \(h_1 = h_f \approx 251.4\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
  - \(v_1 = v_f \approx 0.001017\ \text{m}^3/\text{kg}\)

- Pump to 8.8 MPa:
  \[
  w_{pump,s}=v_1(P_2-P_1)
  =0.001017(8800-20)\approx 8.93\ \text{kJ/kg}
  \]
  \[
  h_{2s}=h_1+w_{pump,s}\approx 260.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
  \]
  With \(\eta_{pump}=w_{s}/w_{actual}=0.82\),
  \[
  w_{pump}=\frac{8.93}{0.82}\approx 10.9\ \text{kJ/kg}
  \]
  \[
  h_2=h_1+w_{pump}\approx 262.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
  \]

- Turbine inlet, state 3, at 8.8 MPa and 576.0°C (superheated steam table):
  \[
  h_3 \approx 3571.0\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_3 \approx 6.95\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
  \]

- Isentropic turbine exit to 20.0 kPa:
  \[
  s_{4s}=s_3
  \]
  At 20.0 kPa, using saturated properties:
  \[
  s_f\approx 0.832,\quad s_{fg}\approx 7.08,\quad h_f\approx 251.4,\quad h_{fg}\approx 2358.3
  \]
  \[
  x_{4s}=\frac{s_3-s_f}{s_{fg}}\approx \frac{6.95-0.832}{7.08}\approx 0.865
  \]
  \[
  h_{4s}=h_f+x_{4s}h_{fg}\approx 2290.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
  \]

- With \(\eta_{turb}=w_{actual}/w_s=0.88\):
  \[
  w_{turb}=0.88(h_3-h_{4s})
  \]
  \[
  h_4=h_3-w_{turb}\approx 2443.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
  \]

- Per unit mass:
  \[
  q_{in}=h_3-h_2
  \]
  \[
  w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{pump}
  \]
  \[
  \eta_{th}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}}
  \]
  \[
  \dot W_{net}=\dot m\, w_{net}
  \]

Final answers:

h₁ = 251.4 kJ/kg  
h₂s = 260.3 kJ/kg  
h₂ = 262.3 kJ/kg  
h₃ = 3571.0 kJ/kg  
h₄s = 2290.0 kJ/kg  
h₄ = 2443.0 kJ/kg  
w_pump = 10.9 kJ/kg  
q_in = 3308.7 kJ/kg  
w_turb = 1128.0 kJ/kg  
w_net = 1117.1 kJ/kg  
η_th = 0.338  
Ẇ_net = 7037.7 kW

question:
An actual Rankine cycle uses water as the working fluid. The condenser pressure is 11.0 kPa and the boiler operates at 11.9 MPa. Steam enters the turbine at 576.0°C. The pump isentropic efficiency is 81.0% and the turbine isentropic efficiency is 80.0%. The mass flow rate is 97.5 kg/s.

Note: η_pump = w_pump,s / w_pump,actual and η_turb = w_turb,actual / w_turb,s.

Determine:
(a) The specific enthalpy at each state point (h₁, h₂s, h₂, h₃, h₄s, h₄)
(b) The pump work, heat input, turbine work, and net work per unit mass
(c) The thermal efficiency and net power output

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂s = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg
h₄s = ___ kJ/kg
h₄ = ___ kJ/kg
w_pump = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
w_turb = ___ kJ/kg
w_net = ___ kJ/kg
η_th = ___
Ẇ_net = ___ kW
raw_response:
Using steam-table properties and the given component efficiencies:

- State 1 is saturated liquid at \(P_1=11.0\ \text{kPa}\)
- State 3 is superheated steam at \(P_3=11.9\ \text{MPa},\ T_3=576.0^\circ\text{C}\)

Key relations used:

\[
w_{\text{pump},s}=v_1(P_2-P_1),\qquad
\eta_{\text{pump}}=\frac{w_{\text{pump},s}}{w_{\text{pump}}}
\]

\[
h_{2s}=h_1+w_{\text{pump},s},\qquad
h_2=h_1+w_{\text{pump}}
\]

\[
s_{4s}=s_3,\qquad
\eta_{\text{turb}}=\frac{w_{\text{turb}}}{w_{\text{turb},s}}
=\frac{h_3-h_4}{h_3-h_{4s}}
\]

\[
q_{in}=h_3-h_2,\qquad
w_{net}=w_{\text{turb}}-w_{\text{pump}},\qquad
\eta_{th}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}}
\]

Using interpolated steam-table values:

- At \(11.0\ \text{kPa}\): \(h_1 \approx 199.8\ \text{kJ/kg}\), \(v_1 \approx 0.00101\ \text{m}^3/\text{kg}\)
- At \(11.9\ \text{MPa},\ 576^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_3 \approx 3546.0\ \text{kJ/kg}\), \(s_3 \approx 6.71\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- Isentropic expansion to \(11.0\ \text{kPa}\) gives \(h_{4s} \approx 2137.0\ \text{kJ/kg}\)

Then:

\[
w_{\text{pump},s}\approx 12.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
w_{\text{pump}}=\frac{12.0}{0.81}\approx 14.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
h_{2s}=199.8+12.0=211.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
h_2=199.8+14.8=214.6\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
w_{\text{turb}}=0.80(3546.0-2137.0)=1127.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
h_4=3546.0-1127.2=2418.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
q_{in}=3546.0-214.6=3331.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
w_{net}=1127.2-14.8=1112.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
\eta_{th}=\frac{1112.4}{3331.4}\approx 0.334
\]

\[
\dot W_{net}= \dot m\, w_{net} = 97.5(1112.4)\approx 108459\ \text{kW}
\]

Final answers:

h₁ = 199.8 kJ/kg  
h₂s = 211.8 kJ/kg  
h₂ = 214.6 kJ/kg  
h₃ = 3546.0 kJ/kg  
h₄s = 2137.0 kJ/kg  
h₄ = 2418.8 kJ/kg  
w_pump = 14.8 kJ/kg  
q_in = 3331.4 kJ/kg  
w_turb = 1127.2 kJ/kg  
w_net = 1112.4 kJ/kg  
η_th = 0.334  
Ẇ_net = 108459 kW

If you want, I can also provide the full \(T\!-\!s\) diagram interpretation for the six state values.
response_text:
Using steam-table properties and the given component efficiencies:

- State 1 is saturated liquid at \(P_1=11.0\ \text{kPa}\)
- State 3 is superheated steam at \(P_3=11.9\ \text{MPa},\ T_3=576.0^\circ\text{C}\)

Key relations used:

\[
w_{\text{pump},s}=v_1(P_2-P_1),\qquad
\eta_{\text{pump}}=\frac{w_{\text{pump},s}}{w_{\text{pump}}}
\]

\[
h_{2s}=h_1+w_{\text{pump},s},\qquad
h_2=h_1+w_{\text{pump}}
\]

\[
s_{4s}=s_3,\qquad
\eta_{\text{turb}}=\frac{w_{\text{turb}}}{w_{\text{turb},s}}
=\frac{h_3-h_4}{h_3-h_{4s}}
\]

\[

\]

\[

\]

\[

\[

\]

\]

\[

At state 3 \((13.4\ \text{MPa},\ 579^\circ\text{C})\), from superheated steam tables:

question:
A steam power plant operates on the Rankine cycle. Water leaves the condenser as saturated liquid at 22.0 kPa and is pumped to 6.6 MPa. The turbine inlet temperature is 559.0°C. The pump has an isentropic efficiency of 84.0% and the turbine has an isentropic efficiency of 87.0%. The mass flow rate is 40.5 kg/s.

Calculate h₁, h₂s, h₂, h₃, h₄s, h₄, w_pump, q_in, w_turb, w_net, η_th, and Ẇ_net.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂s = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg

q_in = ___ kJ/kg
w_turb = ___ kJ/kg
w_net = ___ kJ/kg
η_th = ___
Ẇ_net = ___ kW
raw_response:
Using the standard Rankine-state numbering:

- 1: condenser exit, saturated liquid at \(P_1=22.0\ \text{kPa}\)
- 2: pump exit at \(P_2=6.6\ \text{MPa}\)
- 3: turbine inlet at \(6.6\ \text{MPa},\ 559.0^\circ\text{C}\)
- 4: turbine exit at \(22.0\ \text{kPa}\)

From saturated-water data at \(22.0\ \text{kPa}\) and superheated-steam data at \(6.6\ \text{MPa},\ 559^\circ\text{C}\):

- At state 1: \(h_1 \approx 259.7\ \text{kJ/kg}\), \(v_1 \approx 0.001018\ \text{m}^3/\text{kg}\)
- At state 3: \(h_3 \approx 3548.0\ \text{kJ/kg}\), \(s_3 \approx 7.01\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

### 1) Pump
Isentropic pump outlet:
\[
h_{2s}=h_1+v_1(P_2-P_1)
\]
\[
h_{2s}=259.7+(0.001018)(6600-22)\approx 266.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Pump efficiency:
\[
\eta_p=\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
h_2=h_1+\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{\eta_p}
\]
\[
h_2=259.7+\frac{266.4-259.7}{0.84}\approx 267.7\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

So
\[
w_{pump}=h_2-h_1\approx 8.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

### 2) Turbine
For the isentropic turbine exit, \(s_{4s}=s_3\).  
At \(22.0\ \text{kPa}\), using saturation properties:
\[
x_{4s}=\frac{s_3-s_f}{s_{fg}}\approx 0.877
\]
\[
h_{4s}=h_f+x_{4s}h_{fg}\approx 2323.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Turbine efficiency:
\[
\eta_t=\frac{h_3-h_4}{h_3-h_{4s}}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
h_4=h_3-\eta_t(h_3-h_{4s})
\]
\[
h_4=3548.0-0.87(3548.0-2323.3)\approx 2482.5\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

So
\[
w_{turb}=h_3-h_4\approx 1065.5\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

### 3) Heat input, net work, efficiency
\[
q_{in}=h_3-h_2=3548.0-267.7\approx 3280.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{pump}=1065.5-8.0\approx 1057.5\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
\eta_{th}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}}
=\frac{1057.5}{3280.3}\approx 0.3224
\]

### 4) Net power
\[
\dot W_{net}=\dot m\, w_{net}
=(40.5)(1057.5)\approx 4.2829\times 10^4\ \text{kW}
\]

h₄ = 2482.5 kJ/kg

- \(s_{gen,turb}=s_4-s_3\)

question:
A reheat Rankine cycle uses water. The condenser pressure is 40.0 kPa. Steam is generated at 9.2 MPa and 482.0°C, expanded through the HPT to 1.8 MPa, reheated to 515.0°C, then expanded through the LPT to the condenser pressure. η_pump = 84.0%, η_HPT = 82.0%, η_LPT = 85.0%. ṁ = 32.0 kg/s.

Determine:
(a) h₁, h₂s, h₂, h₃, h₄, h₅, h₆
(b) w_pump, q_in, w_HPT, w_LPT, w_net
(c) η_th and Ẇ_net

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂s = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg
h₄ = ___ kJ/kg
h₅ = ___ kJ/kg
h₆ = ___ kJ/kg
w_pump = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
w_HPT = ___ kJ/kg
w_LPT = ___ kJ/kg
w_net = ___ kJ/kg
η_th = ___
Ẇ_net = ___ kW
raw_response:
Using the usual reheat Rankine state numbering:

- 1: condenser exit / pump inlet, saturated liquid at 40.0 kPa  
- 2s: isentropic pump exit at 9.2 MPa  
- 2: actual pump exit  
- 3: boiler exit / HPT inlet at 9.2 MPa, 482.0°C  
- 4: actual HPT exit at 1.8 MPa  
- 5: reheater exit / LPT inlet at 1.8 MPa, 515.0°C  
- 6: actual LPT exit at 40.0 kPa  

From steam tables/interpolation:

- At 40.0 kPa, saturated liquid: \(h_1 \approx 317.7\ \text{kJ/kg}\), \(v_f \approx 0.001026\ \text{m}^3/\text{kg}\)
- Pump:
  \[
  h_{2s}=h_1+v_f(P_2-P_1)
  \]
  \[
  h_2=h_1+\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{\eta_{pump}}
  \]
- At 9.2 MPa, 482.0°C: \(h_3 \approx 3334.5\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- HPT:
  \[
  \eta_{HPT}=\frac{h_3-h_4}{h_3-h_{4s}}
  \]
  with \(s_{4s}=s_3\) at 1.8 MPa, giving \(h_{4s}\approx 2916.8\ \text{kJ/kg}\), so
  \[
  h_4=h_3-\eta_{HPT}(h_3-h_{4s})
  \]
- At 1.8 MPa, 515.0°C: \(h_5 \approx 3504.7\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- LPT:
  \[
  \eta_{LPT}=\frac{h_5-h_6}{h_5-h_{6s}}

  \]
  giving \(h_6\approx 2380.9\).

Using steam-table values for the superheated states and the compressed-liquid approximation for the pump:

- State 1: saturated liquid at 37.0 kPa  
- Pump: \(h_{2s}\approx h_1+v_1(P_2-P_1)\), \( \eta_{pump}=(h_{2s}-h_1)/(h_2-h_1)\)
- HPT: \( \eta_{HPT}=(h_3-h_4)/(h_3-h_{4s})\)
- LPT: \( \eta_{LPT}=(h_5-h_6)/(h_5-h_{6s})\)

I used \(h_0 \approx 104.83\ \text{kJ/kg}\), \(s_0 \approx 0.3672\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\) at the dead state \((25^\circ\text{C}, 0.1\ \text{MPa})\), and
\[
ef=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0), \qquad x_{dest}=T_0 s_{gen}
\]
with \(T_0=298.15\ \text{K}\).

For entropy generation:
- Pump, HPT, LPT are adiabatic: \(s_{gen}=s_{out}-s_{in}\)
- Boiler/reheater: \(s_{gen}=(s_{out}-s_{in})-q/T_H\)
- Condenser: \(s_{gen}=(s_1-s_6)-q_{cond}/T_L\), with \(q_{cond}=h_1-h_6<0\)

I report \(\eta_{II}\) as the usual heat-engine second-law efficiency \( \eta_{th}/(1-T_L/T_H)\).

question:
Consider an ideal air-standard Brayton cycle (c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)). Compressor inlet: 296.0 K, 104.0 kPa. Pressure ratio: r_p = 17.0. Turbine inlet temperature: 1197.0 K. ṁ = 63.8 kg/s. Both compressor and turbine are isentropic.

Calculate h₁, h₂, h₃, h₄, w_comp, q_in, w_turb, w_net, η_th, and Ẇ_net.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg
h₄ = ___ kJ/kg
w_comp = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
w_turb = ___ kJ/kg
w_net = ___ kJ/kg
η_th = ___
Ẇ_net = ___ kW
raw_response:
For an ideal air-standard Brayton cycle with constant specific heats,

\[
n=\frac{k-1}{k}=\frac{1.4-1}{1.4}=0.285714
\]

Isentropic compressor:
\[
T_2=T_1 r_p^n = 296.0(17.0)^{0.285714}=665.04\ \text{K}
\]

Isentropic turbine:
\[
T_4=\frac{T_3}{r_p^n}=\frac{1197.0}{(17.0)^{0.285714}}=532.77\ \text{K}
\]

Using
\[
h=c_p T
\]

question:
An ideal Brayton cycle uses air (c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)). Compressor inlet: 303.0 K, 96.0 kPa. r_p = 8.0. T₃ = 1181.0 K. ṁ = 48.9 kg/s. Isentropic compressor and turbine. Heat source: 1314.5 K, heat sink: 311.4 K.

Determine all state enthalpies and entropies, component works, heat transfers, thermal efficiency, net power, and entropy generation in each component (compressor, combustion chamber, turbine, heat rejection) and total.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg
h₄ = ___ kJ/kg
w_comp = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
w_turb = ___ kJ/kg
w_net = ___ kJ/kg
η_th = ___
Ẇ_net = ___ kW
s₁ = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s₂ = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s₃ = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s₄ = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,comp = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,cc = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,turb = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,hr = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,total = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
Using the air-standard Brayton-cycle relations with constant \( c_p \) and \( k \):

\[
\frac{T_2}{T_1}=r_p^{(k-1)/k}, \qquad \frac{T_4}{T_3}=r_p^{-(k-1)/k}
\]

Given:

- \(T_1=303.0\ \mathrm{K}\)
- \(P_1=96.0\ \mathrm{kPa}\)
- \(r_p=8.0\)
- \(T_3=1181.0\ \mathrm{K}\)
- \(c_p=1.005\ \mathrm{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}\)
- \(k=1.4\)
- \(R=0.287\ \mathrm{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}\)
- \(\dot m=48.9\ \mathrm{kg/s}\)
- \(T_H=1314.5\ \mathrm{K}\)
- \(T_L=311.4\ \mathrm{K}\)

First, the isentropic temperatures:

\[
T_2 = 303.0(8)^{(1.4-1)/1.4} = 548.87\ \mathrm{K}
\]

\[
T_4 = 1181.0(8)^{-(1.4-1)/1.4} = 651.96\ \mathrm{K}
\]

Enthalpies from \(h=c_pT\):

\[
h_1=1.005(303.0)=304.515
\]
\[
h_2=1.005(548.87)=551.613
\]
\[
h_3=1.005(1181.0)=1186.905
\]
\[
h_4=1.005(651.96)=655.225
\]

Specific works and heats:

\[
w_{comp}=h_2-h_1=247.098
\]

\[
q_{in}=h_3-h_2=635.292
\]

\[
w_{turb}=h_3-h_4=531.680
\]

\[
w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{comp}=284.582
\]

\[
\eta_{th}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}}=0.44795
\]

\[
\dot W_{net}=\dot m\, w_{net}=48.9(284.582)=13916.1\ \mathrm{kW}
\]

Also,

\[
q_{out}=h_4-h_1=350.710\ \mathrm{kJ/kg}
\]

For entropy, only differences are meaningful with constant-\(c_p\) air, so I take state 1 as the reference:

\[
s_1=0
\]

Compressor is isentropic:

\[
s_2-s_1=c_p\ln\!\frac{T_2}{T_1}-R\ln\!\frac{P_2}{P_1}=0
\Rightarrow s_2=0
\]

Combustor is at constant pressure:

\[
s_3-s_2=c_p\ln\!\frac{T_3}{T_2}=0.7701
\Rightarrow s_3=0.7701
\]

Turbine is isentropic:

\[
s_4=s_3=0.7701
\]

Entropy generation per unit mass in each component:

- Compressor (adiabatic, reversible):  
\[
s_{gen,comp}=0
\]

- Combustion chamber:
\[
s_{gen,cc}=(s_3-s_2)-\frac{q_{in}}{T_H}
=0.7701-\frac{635.292}{1314.5}
=0.2868
\]

- Turbine (adiabatic, reversible):  
\[
s_{gen,turb}=0
\]

- Heat rejection:
\[
s_{gen,hr}=(s_1-s_4)+\frac{q_{out}}{T_L}
=-0.7701+\frac{350.710}{311.4}
=0.3561
\]

Total:

\[
s_{gen,total}=0+0.2868+0+0.3561=0.6429
\]

h₂ = 551.613 kJ/kg
h₃ = 1186.905 kJ/kg
h₄ = 655.225 kJ/kg

question:
An ideal Brayton cycle uses air (c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)). Compressor inlet: 304.0 K, 104.0 kPa. r_p = 13.0. T₃ = 1114.0 K. ṁ = 143.2 kg/s. Isentropic compressor and turbine. Heat source: 1266.2 K, heat sink: 313.7 K. Dead state: T₀ = 298.15 K, P₀ = 100 kPa.

Determine all state enthalpies, entropies, and flow exergies. Calculate component works, heat transfers, thermal efficiency, net power. Find entropy generation and exergy destruction in each component, total exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg

w_comp = ___ kJ/kg

s_gen,hr = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,total = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
ef₁ = ___ kJ/kg
ef₂ = ___ kJ/kg
ef₃ = ___ kJ/kg
ef₄ = ___ kJ/kg
x_dest,cc = ___ kJ/kg
x_dest,hr = ___ kJ/kg
x_dest,total = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using ideal-gas air with constant properties:

- \(h = c_p T\)
- \(s - s_0 = c_p \ln(T/T_0) - R \ln(P/P_0)\), with \(s_0=0\) at the dead state
- \(ef = (h-h_0) - T_0(s-s_0)\), where \(h_0=c_pT_0\)

For the isentropic compressor and turbine:

\[
T_2=T_1 r_p^{(k-1)/k},\qquad
T_4=T_3 r_p^{-(k-1)/k}
\]

With \(r_p=13\), \(k=1.4\):

\[
T_2 \approx 632.61\text{ K},\qquad T_4 \approx 535.32\text{ K}
\]

Then:

- \(h_i=c_pT_i\)
- \(w_{comp}=h_2-h_1\)
- \(q_{in}=h_3-h_2\)
- \(w_{turb}=h_3-h_4\)
- \(w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{comp}\)
- \(\eta_{th}=w_{net}/q_{in}\)

Entropy generation per unit mass:

- Compressor: \(s_{gen,comp}=0\)
- Combustor/heater:
  \[
  s_{gen,cc}= (s_3-s_2)-\frac{q_{in}}{T_H}
  \]
- Turbine: \(s_{gen,turb}=0\)
- Heat rejector:
  \[
  s_{gen,hr}= (s_1-s_4)+\frac{q_{out}}{T_C},
  \quad q_{out}=h_4-h_1
  \]

Exergy destruction:

\[
x_{dest}=T_0 s_{gen}
\]

Second-law efficiency for the cycle was taken as

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{w_{net}}{(1-T_0/T_H)q_{in}-(1-T_0/T_C)q_{out}}
\]

Final answers:

question:
A gas turbine power plant operates on the Brayton cycle with air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4). Air enters the compressor at 299.0 K and 104.0 kPa with a pressure ratio of 12.0. The turbine inlet temperature is 1368.0 K. The compressor isentropic efficiency is 88.0% and the turbine isentropic efficiency is 85.0%. ṁ = 103.1 kg/s.

Calculate all state enthalpies, component works, net work, thermal efficiency, and net power.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂s = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg
h₄s = ___ kJ/kg

η_th = ___
Ẇ_net = ___ kW
raw_response:
Using the ideal-gas Brayton-cycle relations with constant specific heats:

- \( h = c_p T \)
- \( \dfrac{T_{2s}}{T_1} = \left(\dfrac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k} \)
- \( \eta_c = \dfrac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1} = \dfrac{T_{2s}-T_1}{T_2-T_1} \)
- \( \dfrac{T_{4s}}{T_3} = \left(\dfrac{P_4}{P_3}\right)^{(k-1)/k} \)
- \( \eta_t = \dfrac{h_3-h_4}{h_3-h_{4s}} = \dfrac{T_3-T_4}{T_3-T_{4s}} \)

Given:
- \( c_p = 1.005 \,\text{kJ/(kg·K)} \)
- \( k = 1.4 \)
- \( T_1 = 299.0 \,\text{K} \)
- \( P_2/P_1 = 12.0 \)
- \( T_3 = 1368.0 \,\text{K} \)
- \( \eta_c = 0.88 \)
- \( \eta_t = 0.85 \)
- \( \dot m = 103.1 \,\text{kg/s} \)

From these:
- \( T_{2s} \approx 608.16 \,\text{K} \)
- \( T_2 \approx 650.32 \,\text{K} \)
- \( T_{4s} \approx 672.57 \,\text{K} \)
- \( T_4 \approx 776.88 \,\text{K} \)

Final answers:

h₂s = 611.204 kJ/kg  
h₂ = 653.573 kJ/kg

Note: η_comp = w_comp,s / w_comp,actual, η_turb = w_turb,actual / w_turb,s.

Determine:
(a) h₁, h₂s, h₂, h₃, h₄s, h₄
(b) w_comp, q_in, w_turb, w_net
(c) η_th and Ẇ_net

η_th = ___
Ẇ_net = ___ kW
raw_response:
Using the cold-air-standard relations with constant \( c_p \) and \( k \):

\[
h = c_p T,\qquad n=\frac{k-1}{k}=\frac{0.4}{1.4}=0.285714
\]

\[
P_2=P_3=r_p P_1=12(104)=1248\ \text{kPa},\qquad P_4=P_1
\]

Isentropic compressor outlet temperature:

\[
T_{2s}=T_1 r_p^n=292(12)^{0.285714}=593.91\ \text{K}
\]

Compressor efficiency:

\[
\eta_{comp}=\frac{T_{2s}-T_1}{T_2-T_1}
\Rightarrow
T_2=T_1+\frac{T_{2s}-T_1}{\eta_{comp}}
=651.42\ \text{K}
\]

Isentropic turbine outlet temperature:

\[
T_{4s}=T_3\left(\frac{1}{r_p}\right)^n
=1377(12)^{-0.285714}=677.01\ \text{K}
\]

Turbine efficiency:

\[
\eta_{turb}=\frac{T_3-T_4}{T_3-T_{4s}}
\Rightarrow
T_4=T_3-\eta_{turb}(T_3-T_{4s})
=775.01\ \text{K}
\]

Then:

\[
h_i=c_p T_i
\]

\[
w_{comp}=h_2-h_1,\quad
q_{in}=h_3-h_2,\quad
w_{turb}=h_3-h_4,\quad
w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{comp}
\]

\[
\eta_{th}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}},\qquad
\dot W_{net}=\dot m\, w_{net}
\]

Using the air-standard Brayton-cycle relations with constant properties:

- \(P_2=P_3=r_p P_1=11(97)=1067\ \text{kPa}\), and \(P_4=P_1=97\ \text{kPa}\)
- Isentropic compressor outlet:
  \[
  T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
  \]
- Actual compressor outlet:
  \[
  \eta_{comp}=\frac{T_{2s}-T_1}{T_2-T_1}
  \]
- Isentropic turbine outlet:
  \[
  T_{4s}=T_3\left(\frac{P_4}{P_3}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
  \]
- Actual turbine outlet:
  \[
  \eta_{turb}=\frac{T_3-T_4}{T_3-T_{4s}}
  \]
- Enthalpy:
  \[
  h=c_p T
  \]
- Entropy relative to the dead state \((T_0,P_0)\):
  \[
  s=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T}{T_0}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P}{P_0}\right)
  \]
- Flow exergy:
  \[
  ef=(h-h_0)-T_0 s,\qquad h_0=c_pT_0
  \]
- Heat/work per unit mass:
  \[
  w_{comp}=h_2-h_1,\quad q_{in}=h_3-h_2,\quad w_{turb}=h_3-h_4
  \]
  \[
  w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{comp},\quad \eta_{th}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}}
  \]
- Entropy generation:
  \[
  s_{gen,comp}=s_2-s_1
  \]
  \[
  s_{gen,cc}=(s_3-s_2)-\frac{q_{in}}{T_{source}}
  \]
  \[
  s_{gen,turb}=s_4-s_3
  \]
  \[
  s_{gen,hr}=(s_1-s_4)+\frac{q_{out}}{T_{sink}},\quad q_{out}=h_4-h_1
  \]
- Exergy destruction:
  \[
  x_{dest}=T_0 s_{gen}
  \]

I take the second-law efficiency as the exergetic efficiency of the cycle:
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}\left(1-\frac{T_0}{T_{source}}\right)}
\]

Using the constant-\(c_p\) ideal-gas Brayton-cycle relations:

- \(h = c_p T\)
- Compressor isentropic: \(T_{2s} = T_1 r_p^{(k-1)/k}\)
- Compressor efficiency: \(\eta_{comp} = \dfrac{T_{2s}-T_1}{T_2-T_1}\)
- Turbine isentropic: \(T_{5s} = T_4 / r_p^{(k-1)/k}\)
- Turbine efficiency: \(\eta_{turb} = \dfrac{T_4-T_5}{T_4-T_{5s}}\)
- Regenerator effectiveness: \(\varepsilon = \dfrac{T_3-T_2}{T_5-T_2}\)
- Regenerator energy balance: \(T_6 = T_5-(T_3-T_2)\)

q_{in}=h_4-h_3,\quad
w_{turb}=h_4-h_5,\quad
w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{comp}
\]
\[
\eta_{th}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}},\quad

\]

\]
- Thermal efficiency:

\]

Key temperature relations:

- Compressor isentropic: \(T_{2s}=T_1 r_p^{(k-1)/k}\)

- Turbine isentropic: \(T_{5s}=T_4/r_p^{(k-1)/k}\)
- Turbine efficiency: \(\eta_{turb}=(T_4-T_5)/(T_4-T_{5s})\)
- Regenerator effectiveness: \(\varepsilon=(T_3-T_2)/(T_5-T_2)\), with \(T_6=T_5-(T_3-T_2)\)

- \(w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{comp}\)
- \(\eta_{th}=w_{net}/q_{in}\)
- \(\dot W_{net}=\dot m\, w_{net}\)

For entropy, using the constant-\(c_p\) ideal-gas relation and taking \(s_1=0\) as the reference:

\[
s_i-s_1=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_i}{T_1}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_i}{P_1}\right)

Entropy generation per unit mass:

- Compressor: \(s_{gen,comp}=s_2-s_1\)
- Regenerator: \(s_{gen,regen}=(s_3+s_6)-(s_2+s_5)\)
- Combustion chamber: \(s_{gen,cc}=(s_4-s_3)-q_{in}/T_H\)
- Turbine: \(s_{gen,turb}=s_5-s_4\)
- Heat rejection: \(s_{gen,hr}=(s_1-s_6)+q_{out}/T_C\), with \(q_{out}=h_6-h_1\)

Using ideal-gas air with constant properties and negligible pressure losses outside the compressor/turbine:

- \(P_2=P_3=P_4=r_p P_1=816\ \text{kPa}\)
- \(P_5=P_6=P_1=102\ \text{kPa}\)

Key relations used:
- Isentropic compressor/turbine:
  \[
  T_{2s}=T_1 r_p^{(k-1)/k},\qquad
  T_{5s}=T_4 r_p^{-(k-1)/k}
  \]
- Component efficiencies:
  \[
  \eta_{comp}=\frac{T_{2s}-T_1}{T_2-T_1},\qquad
  \eta_{turb}=\frac{T_4-T_5}{T_4-T_{5s}}
  \]
- Regenerator effectiveness:
  \[
  \varepsilon=\frac{T_3-T_2}{T_5-T_2}
  \]
- Constant-\(c_p\) enthalpy:
  \[
  h=c_p T
  \]
- Entropy relative to dead state \((T_0,P_0)\):
  \[
  s-cst = c_p\ln\!\frac{T}{T_0}-R\ln\!\frac{P}{P_0}
  \]
  (reported below as \(s_1,\dots,s_6\), relative to the dead state)
- Flow exergy:
  \[
  ef=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
  \]
- Entropy generation:
  \[
  s_{gen,comp}=s_2-s_1,\quad
  s_{gen,turb}=s_5-s_4
  \]
  \[
  s_{gen,regen}=(s_3-s_2)+(s_6-s_5)
  \]
  \[
  s_{gen,cc}=s_4-s_3-\frac{q_{in}}{T_H}
  \]
  \[
  s_{gen,hr}=s_1-s_6+\frac{q_{out}}{T_C}
  \]
- Exergy destruction:
  \[
  x_{dest}=T_0 s_{gen}
  \]
- Second-law efficiency (based on exergy of heat supplied from the source):
  \[
  \eta_{II}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}\left(1-\frac{T_0}{T_H}\right)}
  \]

question:
An actual vapor-compression refrigeration cycle uses R-134a. The evaporator temperature is -16.0°C and the condenser temperature is 48.0°C. The compressor has an isentropic efficiency of 83.0%. The mass flow rate of refrigerant is 0.248 kg/s. The refrigerant leaves the evaporator as saturated vapor and leaves the condenser as saturated liquid. The expansion valve is isenthalpic.

Use the IIR reference state for R-134a properties: h = 200.0 kJ/kg and s = 1.0000 kJ/(kg·K) for saturated liquid at 0°C.

Determine:
(a) h₁, h₂s, h₂, h₃, h₄
(b) w_comp, q_L (cooling effect), q_H (heat rejection)
(c) Quality after throttle (x₄), COP_R, Ẇ_comp, and Q̇_L

Present your final answers in the following format:

h₂s = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg
h₄ = ___ kJ/kg
w_comp = ___ kJ/kg

q_H = ___ kJ/kg
x₄ = ___

raw_response:
Using R-134a tables with the IIR reference state:

- State 1: saturated vapor at \(T_{evap}=-16.0^\circ\text{C}\)
- State 3: saturated liquid at \(T_{cond}=48.0^\circ\text{C}\)
- Throttle valve: \(h_4=h_3\)
- Compressor isentropic efficiency:
\[
\eta_c=\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
h_2=h_1+\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{\eta_c}
\]
- Refrigeration quantities:
\[
w_{comp}=h_2-h_1,\quad
q_L=h_1-h_4,\quad
q_H=h_2-h_3
\]

question:
A refrigeration system operates on the vapor-compression cycle with R-134a. Operating conditions: T_evap = -3.0°C, T_cond = 34.0°C. The compressor isentropic efficiency is 86.0%. ṁ = 0.345 kg/s. State 1: saturated vapor at evaporator. State 3: saturated liquid at condenser. Throttle is isenthalpic (h₄ = h₃).

Use the IIR reference state for R-134a properties: h = 200.0 kJ/kg and s = 1.0000 kJ/(kg·K) for saturated liquid at 0°C.

Calculate all state enthalpies, compressor work, cooling effect, heat rejection, throttle exit quality (x₄), COP_R, compressor power, and cooling capacity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂s = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg
h₄ = ___ kJ/kg
w_comp = ___ kJ/kg
q_L = ___ kJ/kg
q_H = ___ kJ/kg
x₄ = ___
COP_R = ___
Ẇ_comp = ___ kW
Q̇_L = ___ kW
raw_response:
Using R-134a properties on the IIR basis:

- State 1 (sat. vapor at \(T_{\text{evap}}=-3^\circ\text{C}\)):  
  \(h_1 \approx 396.8\ \text{kJ/kg}\), \(s_1 \approx 1.728\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

- State 3 (sat. liquid at \(T_{\text{cond}}=34^\circ\text{C}\)):  
  \(h_3 \approx 248.0\ \text{kJ/kg}\)

- State 2s: isentropic compression to condenser pressure, so \(s_{2s}=s_1\).  
  From superheated R-134a data at \(P_{\text{cond}}\), \(h_{2s} \approx 420.9\ \text{kJ/kg}\)

Compressor isentropic efficiency:
\[
\eta_c=\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1}
\]
\[
h_2=h_1+\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{\eta_c}
=396.8+\frac{420.9-396.8}{0.86}
\approx 424.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Throttle valve is isenthalpic:
\[
h_4=h_3=248.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Specific quantities:
\[
w_{\text{comp}}=h_2-h_1=424.8-396.8=28.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
q_L=h_1-h_4=396.8-248.0=148.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
q_H=h_2-h_3=424.8-248.0=176.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Throttle-exit quality at evaporator pressure:
\[
x_4=\frac{h_4-h_f(-3^\circ\text{C})}{h_g(-3^\circ\text{C})-h_f(-3^\circ\text{C})}
\]
Using \(h_f(-3^\circ\text{C})\approx 195.7\ \text{kJ/kg}\) and \(h_g(-3^\circ\text{C})=h_1\approx 396.8\ \text{kJ/kg}\),
\[
x_4=\frac{248.0-195.7}{396.8-195.7}\approx 0.260
\]

Refrigeration COP:
\[
\text{COP}_R=\frac{q_L}{w_{\text{comp}}}
=\frac{148.8}{28.0}\approx 5.31
\]

With \(\dot m=0.345\ \text{kg/s}\):
\[
\dot W_{\text{comp}}=\dot m\, w_{\text{comp}}
=0.345(28.0)\approx 9.66\ \text{kW}
\]
\[
\dot Q_L=\dot m\, q_L
=0.345(148.8)\approx 51.3\ \text{kW}
\]

Using R-134a tables with the **IIR reference state**:

- **State 1:** saturated vapor at \(T_{\text{evap}}=-10^\circ\text{C}\)
- **State 3:** saturated liquid at \(T_{\text{cond}}=34^\circ\text{C}\)
- **State 4:** throttling, so \(h_4=h_3\)
- **State 2s:** isentropic compression to condenser pressure, so \(s_{2s}=s_1\)
- **State 2:** actual compressor outlet from  
\[
\eta_{\text{comp}}=\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1}
\]

From the R-134a tables/interpolation:

- At \(-10^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_g\approx 392.6\), \(s_g\approx 1.7316\), \(h_f\approx 186.7\), \(s_f\approx 0.9485\)
- At \(34^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_f\approx 249.0\), \(s_f\approx 1.1670\)

Then:

\[
h_2 = h_1 + \frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{\eta_{\text{comp}}}
\]

\[
w_{\text{comp}} = h_2-h_1,\quad
q_L = h_1-h_4,\quad
q_H = h_2-h_3
\]

\[
x_4=\frac{h_4-h_f@(-10^\circ\text{C})}{h_g@(-10^\circ\text{C})-h_f@(-10^\circ\text{C})}
\]

\[
\text{COP}_R=\frac{q_L}{w_{\text{comp}}}
\]

Rates:

\[
\dot W_{\text{comp}}=\dot m\, w_{\text{comp}},\qquad
\dot Q_L=\dot m\, q_L
\]

Entropy generation per unit mass:

- Compressor (adiabatic):
\[
s_{\text{gen,comp}}=s_2-s_1
\]

- Condenser with sink at \(T_0=293.2\text{ K}\):
\[
s_{\text{gen,cond}}=(s_3-s_2)+\frac{q_H}{T_0}
\]

- Throttle valve (adiabatic):
\[
s_{\text{gen,throttle}}=s_4-s_3
\]

- Evaporator with cooled space at \(T_L=272.0\text{ K}\):
\[
s_{\text{gen,evap}}=(s_1-s_4)-\frac{q_L}{T_L}
\]

- Total:
\[
s_{\text{gen,total}}=\sum s_{\text{gen}}=\frac{q_H}{293.2}-\frac{q_L}{272.0}
\]

Also, the condenser heat rejection rate is \(\dot Q_H=\dot m q_H \approx 23.3\text{ kW}\).

\]

- Evaporator with cooled space at \(T_L=272.0\text{ K}\):
\[
s_{\text{gen,evap}}=(s_1-s_4)-\frac{q_L}{T_L}
\]

- Total:
\[
s_{\text{gen,total}}=\sum s_{\text{gen}}=\frac{q_H}{293.2}-\frac{q_L}{272.0}
\]

Also, the condenser heat rejection rate is \(\dot Q_H=\dot m q_H \approx 23.3\text{ kW}\).

Use the IIR reference state for R-134a properties: h = 200.0 kJ/kg and s = 1.0000 kJ/(kg·K) for saturated liquid at 0°C.

Determine all state enthalpies, entropies, compressor work, cooling effect, heat rejection, x₄, COP_R, power and capacity. Also find entropy generation in each component (compressor, condenser, throttle valve, evaporator) and total entropy generation per unit mass.

raw_response:
Using the standard VCR state numbering:

- 1: evaporator exit / compressor inlet  
- 2s: compressor exit if isentropic  
- 2: actual compressor exit  
- 3: condenser exit / valve inlet  
- 4: valve exit / evaporator inlet

Using R-134a tables on the **IIR basis**:

- At \(T_{evap}=-15^\circ C\): saturated vapor at state 1, and saturated-liquid/vapor mixture data for state 4  
- At \(T_{cond}=35^\circ C\): saturated liquid at state 3  
- Approximate saturation pressures used from tables:  
  \(P_{evap}\approx 0.164\ \text{MPa}\), \(P_{cond}\approx 0.887\ \text{MPa}\)

Property data used from the tables/interpolation:

- At \(-15^\circ C\):  
  \(h_g \approx 389.6\), \(s_g \approx 1.736\), \(h_f \approx 178.1\), \(s_f \approx 0.917\),  
  \(h_{fg}\approx 211.5\), \(s_{fg}\approx 0.819\)
- At \(35^\circ C\):  
  saturated liquid \(h_f \approx 249.1\), \(s_f \approx 1.167\)

Calculations:

1. **State 1**
   \[
   h_1=h_g(-15^\circ C),\qquad s_1=s_g(-15^\circ C)
   \]

2. **State 2s**  
   Isentropic compression:
   \[
   s_{2s}=s_1
   \]
   At \(P_2=P_{cond}\), superheated-table interpolation gives \(h_{2s}\approx 424.4\ \text{kJ/kg}\).

3. **State 2**
   \[
   \eta_{comp}=\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1}
   \]
   \[
   h_2=h_1+\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{\eta_{comp}}
   \]
   Then at \(P_2=P_{cond}\), \(h_2\) gives \(s_2\approx 1.755\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\).

4. **State 3**
   \[
   h_3=h_f(35^\circ C),\qquad s_3=s_f(35^\circ C)
   \]

5. **State 4**  
   Throttle valve is isenthalpic:
   \[
   h_4=h_3
   \]
   Quality at evaporator pressure:
   \[
   x_4=\frac{h_4-h_f(-15^\circ C)}{h_{fg}(-15^\circ C)}
   \]
   \[
   s_4=s_f(-15^\circ C)+x_4\,s_{fg}(-15^\circ C)
   \]

6. **Energy transfers**
   \[
   w_{comp}=h_2-h_1
   \]
   \[
   q_L=h_1-h_4
   \]
   \[
   q_H=h_2-h_3
   \]
   \[
   COP_R=\frac{q_L}{w_{comp}}
   \]

7. **Rates**
   \[
   \dot W_{comp}=\dot m\, w_{comp}
   \]
   \[
   \dot Q_L=\dot m\, q_L
   \]
   For reference,
   \[
   \dot Q_H=\dot m\, q_H \approx 32.57\ \text{kW}
   \]

8. **Entropy generation per unit mass**
   - Compressor, adiabatic:
     \[
     s_{gen,comp}=s_2-s_1
     \]
   - Condenser, heat rejected to environment at \(T_H=297.6\ \text{K}\):
     \[
     s_{gen,cond}=s_3-s_2+\frac{q_H}{T_H}
     \]
   - Throttle valve, adiabatic:
     \[
     s_{gen,throttle}=s_4-s_3
     \]
   - Evaporator, heat absorbed from cooled space at \(T_L=265.7\ \text{K}\):
     \[
     s_{gen,evap}=s_1-s_4-\frac{q_L}{T_L}
     \]
   - Total:
     \[
     s_{gen,total}=s_{gen,comp}+s_{gen,cond}+s_{gen,throttle}+s_{gen,evap}
     \]

  \]
- Entropy generation per kg:
  \[
  s_{\text{gen,comp}}=s_2-s_1
  \]
  \[
  s_{\text{gen,cond}}=(s_3-s_2)+\frac{q_H}{T_{\text{env}}}
  \]
  \[
  s_{\text{gen,throttle}}=s_4-s_3
  \]
  \[
  s_{\text{gen,evap}}=(s_1-s_4)-\frac{q_L}{T_{\text{space}}}
  \]
- Flow exergy:
  \[
  ef=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
  \]
  with dead-state R-134a at \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C},\,P_0=0.1\text{ MPa}\): \(h_0\approx 427.0\text{ kJ/kg},\, s_0\approx 1.9050\text{ kJ/(kg·K)}\).
- Exergy destruction:
  \[
  x_{\text{dest}}=T_0 s_{\text{gen}}
  \]
- Carnot COP:
  \[
  \text{COP}_{\text{Carnot}}=\frac{T_{\text{space}}}{T_{\text{env}}-T_{\text{space}}}
  \]

An actual Brayton cycle uses air as the working fluid. Use air-standard assumptions with variable specific heats (temperature-dependent properties). Compressor inlet: 290.0 K, 104.0 kPa. Pressure ratio: r_p = 12.0. Turbine inlet: 1142.0 K. η_comp = 82.0%, η_turb = 87.0%. ṁ = 116.7 kg/s.

Note: η_comp = w_comp,s / w_comp,actual, η_turb = w_turb,actual / w_turb,s.

Determine:
(a) h₁, h₂s, h₂, T₂, h₃, h₄s, h₄, T₄
(b) w_comp, q_in, w_turb, w_net
(c) η_th and Ẇ_net

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂s = ___ kJ/kg

h₄ = ___ kJ/kg

q_in = ___ kJ/kg
w_turb = ___ kJ/kg
w_net = ___ kJ/kg
η_th = ___
Ẇ_net = ___ kW
raw_response:
Using ideal-gas air with variable specific heats, and taking enthalpies from the standard air-property reference:

- \(P_2=P_3=r_p P_1=12(104)=1248\ \text{kPa}\)
- \(P_4=P_1=104\ \text{kPa}\)

For the isentropic states, use the variable-\(c_p\) ideal-gas entropy relation:

\[
s^\circ(T_{2s})-s^\circ(T_1)=R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)
\]

\[
s^\circ(T_{4s})-s^\circ(T_3)=R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_4}{P_3}\right)
\]

Then apply the given component efficiencies:

\[
\eta_{comp}=\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1}
\qquad
\eta_{turb}=\frac{h_3-h_4}{h_3-h_{4s}}
\]

and the Brayton-cycle energy balances:

\[
w_{comp}=h_2-h_1,\quad
q_{in}=h_3-h_2,\quad
w_{turb}=h_3-h_4,\quad
w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{comp}
\]

\[
\eta_{th}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}},
\qquad
\dot W_{net}=\dot m\, w_{net}
\]

Final results:

Using the air-standard Brayton-cycle relations with variable specific heats:

- \(p_2/p_1 = p_3/p_4 = r_p = 7\)
- Isentropic compressor:  
  \[
  s_2^s-s_1=0 \;\Rightarrow\; s^\circ(T_{2s})-s^\circ(T_1)=R\ln\!\left(\frac{p_2}{p_1}\right)
  \]
- Compressor efficiency:  
  \[
  \eta_{comp}=\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1}
  \]
- Isentropic turbine:  
  \[
  s_4^s-s_3=0 \;\Rightarrow\; s^\circ(T_{4s})-s^\circ(T_3)=R\ln\!\left(\frac{p_4}{p_3}\right)
  \]
- Turbine efficiency:  
  \[
  \eta_{turb}=\frac{h_3-h_4}{h_3-h_{4s}}
  \]

From variable-\(c_p\) air properties:

- At \(T_1=300.0\) K: \(h_1=300.19\) kJ/kg

- At \(T_3=1327.0\) K: \(h_3\approx 1430.83\) kJ/kg
- Solving the isentropic expansion relation gives \(T_{4s}\approx 816.79\) K, hence \(h_{4s}\approx 841.96\) kJ/kg
- Using \(\eta_{turb}=0.82\): \(h_4=947.95\) kJ/kg, which corresponds to \(T_4\approx 911.68\) K

\[
w_{turb}=h_3-h_4
\]
\[
w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{comp}
\]
\[
\eta_{th}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}}
\]
\[
\dot W_{net}=\dot m\, w_{net}
\]

- \( s_4^s = s_3 \Rightarrow s^\circ(T_{4s}) - s^\circ(T_3) = R\ln(p_4/p_3) \)

- Turbine (adiabatic): \( s_{gen,turb}=s_4-s_3 \)
- Heat rejection: \( s_{gen,hr}=s_1-s_4+\dfrac{q_{out}}{T_{sink}} \), with \( q_{out}=h_4-h_1 \)

question:
A regenerative Brayton cycle uses air with variable specific heats (temperature-dependent properties). Compressor inlet: 292.0 K, 96.0 kPa. Pressure ratio: r_p = 9.0. Turbine inlet temperature (state 4): 1331.0 K. η_comp = 83.0%, η_turb = 83.0%. Regenerator effectiveness: ε = 71.0%. ṁ = 22.1 kg/s.

States: 1→compressor inlet, 2→compressor exit, 3→regenerator cold exit, 4→turbine inlet, 5→turbine exit, 6→regenerator hot exit.
ε = (T₃ - T₂) / (T₅ - T₂).

Determine:
(a) h₁, h₂s, h₂, T₂, h₃, h₄, h₅s, h₅, T₅, h₆
(b) w_comp, q_in, w_turb, w_net
(c) η_th and Ẇ_net

Present your final answers in the following format:

raw_response:
Using ideal-gas air with variable specific heats, the state calculations are based on:

- Isentropic compressor:
  \[
  s_2^s-s_1=\int_{T_1}^{T_{2s}}\frac{c_p(T)}{T}\,dT-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)=0
  \]
- Compressor efficiency:
  \[
  \eta_{comp}=\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1}
  \]
- Isentropic turbine:
  \[
  s_5^s-s_4=\int_{T_4}^{T_{5s}}\frac{c_p(T)}{T}\,dT-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_5}{P_4}\right)=0
  \]
- Turbine efficiency:
  \[
  \eta_{turb}=\frac{h_4-h_5}{h_4-h_{5s}}
  \]
- Regenerator:
  \[
  T_3=T_2+\varepsilon\,(T_5-T_2)
  \]
  and from energy balance,
  \[
  h_6=h_5-(h_3-h_2)
  \]

Then:
\[
w_{comp}=h_2-h_1,\quad
w_{turb}=h_4-h_5,\quad
q_{in}=h_4-h_3
\]
\[
w_{net}=w_{turb}-w_{comp},\quad
\eta_{th}=\frac{w_{net}}{q_{in}},\quad
\dot W_{net}=\dot m\,w_{net}
\]

States: 1→compressor inlet, 2→compressor exit, 3→regenerator cold exit, 4→turbine inlet, 5→turbine exit, 6→regenerator hot exit.
ε = (T₃ - T₂) / (T₅ - T₂).

Determine:
(a) h₁, h₂s, h₂, T₂, h₃, h₄, h₅s, h₅, T₅, h₆
(b) w_comp, q_in, w_turb, w_net

raw_response:
Using ideal-gas air with temperature-dependent properties, the states are found from:

- Compressor isentropic outlet:  
  \( s^\circ(T_{2s}) - s^\circ(T_1) = R \ln\!\left(\dfrac{p_2}{p_1}\right) \)
- Compressor efficiency:  
  \( \eta_{comp} = \dfrac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1} \)
- Turbine isentropic outlet:  
  \( s^\circ(T_{5s}) - s^\circ(T_4) = R \ln\!\left(\dfrac{p_5}{p_4}\right) \)
- Turbine efficiency:  
  \( \eta_{turb} = \dfrac{h_4-h_5}{h_4-h_{5s}} \)
- Regenerator effectiveness:  
  \( T_3 = T_2 + \varepsilon\,(T_5-T_2) \)
- Regenerator energy balance:  
  \( h_6 = h_5 - (h_3-h_2) \)

This gives approximately:
- \(T_{2s} \approx 557.4\ \text{K}\)
- \(T_{5s} \approx 898.1\ \text{K}\)

Using ideal-gas air with temperature-dependent properties, the solution follows:

- Isentropic compressor:  
  \( s_2^s = s_1 \Rightarrow s^\circ(T_{2s})-s^\circ(T_1)=R\ln(P_2/P_1) \)
- Compressor efficiency:  
  \( \eta_{comp}=\dfrac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1} \)
- Isentropic turbine:  
  \( s_5^s = s_4 \Rightarrow s^\circ(T_{5s})-s^\circ(T_4)=R\ln(P_5/P_4) \)
- Turbine efficiency:  
  \( \eta_{turb}=\dfrac{h_4-h_5}{h_4-h_5^s} \)
- Regenerator effectiveness:  
  \( T_3=T_2+\varepsilon\,(T_5-T_2) \)
- Regenerator energy balance:  
  \( h_6=h_5-(h_3-h_2) \)
- Specific transfers:  
  \( w_{comp}=h_2-h_1,\quad w_{turb}=h_4-h_5,\quad q_{in}=h_4-h_3,\quad q_{out}=h_6-h_1 \)
- Thermal efficiency:  
  \( \eta_{th}=w_{net}/q_{in} \)
- Entropy generation:
  - Compressor: \( s_{gen,comp}=s_2-s_1 \)
  - Regenerator: \( s_{gen,regen}=(s_3-s_2)+(s_6-s_5) \)
  - Combustor: \( s_{gen,cc}=(s_4-s_3)-q_{in}/T_H \)
  - Turbine: \( s_{gen,turb}=s_5-s_4 \)
  - Heat rejection: \( s_{gen,hr}=(s_1-s_6)+q_{out}/T_C \)

Intermediate temperatures from the variable-\(c_p\) model:
\(T_{2s}\approx 568.8\ \mathrm{K}\), \(T_3\approx 785.0\ \mathrm{K}\), \(T_{5s}\approx 784.2\ \mathrm{K}\), \(T_6\approx 696.1\ \mathrm{K}\), and \(q_{out}\approx 413.89\ \mathrm{kJ/kg}\).

Dead state: T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa.

Determine all state enthalpies (h₁ through h₉), isentropic references (h₂s, h₄s, h₇s, h₉s), actual temperatures (T₂, T₄), steam mass flow rate (ṁ_steam), component works, heat input, combined net power (W_net_combined), and combined thermal efficiency (η_combined).

raw_response:
Using variable-\(c_p\) air relations on the gas side and steam-table values on the water side:

- Air side: solve the isentropic states from  
  \[
  s_2^o-s_1^o=R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right),\qquad
  s_4^o-s_3^o=R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_4}{P_3}\right)
  \]
  then apply
  \[
  \eta_c=\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1},\qquad
  \eta_t=\frac{h_3-h_4}{h_3-h_{4s}}
  \]
  with standard ideal-gas air enthalpy reference.

- Steam side: at \(P_6=20\) kPa, state 6 is saturated liquid.  
  Pump:
  \[
  h_{7s}\approx h_6+v_f(P_7-P_6),\qquad
  \eta_p=\frac{h_{7s}-h_6}{h_7-h_6}
  \]
  Boiler outlet: \(P_8=5.6\) MPa and \(T_8=T_{\text{sat}}+97^\circ\mathrm{C}\approx 369^\circ\mathrm{C}\), giving \(h_8\) and \(s_8\) from superheated steam tables.  
  Turbine:
  \[
  s_{9s}=s_8,\qquad
  \eta_{st}=\frac{h_8-h_9}{h_8-h_{9s}}
  \]

- HRSG:
  \[
  \dot m_{air}(h_4-h_5)=\dot m_{steam}(h_8-h_7)
  \]

- Combined net power:
  \[
  \dot W_{net,combined}
  =\dot m_{air}\big[(h_3-h_4)-(h_2-h_1)\big]
  +\dot m_{steam}\big[(h_8-h_9)-(h_7-h_6)\big]
  \]

- Combined thermal efficiency:
  \[
  \eta_{combined}=
  \frac{\dot W_{net,combined}}{\dot m_{air}(h_3-h_2)}
  \]

raw_response:
Using variable-\(c_p\) ideal-gas air on the gas side, saturated/superheated steam-table interpolation on the steam side, and neglecting pressure losses in the combustor/HRSG:

Key relations used:
- Compressor: \(\eta_c=\dfrac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1}\)
- Gas turbine: \(\eta_{gt}=\dfrac{h_3-h_4}{h_3-h_{4s}}\)
- Pump: \(h_{7s}\approx h_6+v_f(P_7-P_6)\), \(\eta_p=\dfrac{h_{7s}-h_6}{h_7-h_6}\)
- Steam turbine: \(\eta_{st}=\dfrac{h_8-h_9}{h_8-h_{9s}}\)
- HRSG: \(\dot m_a(h_4-h_5)=\dot m_s(h_8-h_7)\)
- Combined efficiency: \(\eta_{combined}=\dfrac{\dot W_{net,combined}}{\dot m_a q_{combustion}}\)

Entropy generation:
- \(s_{gen,comp}=s_2-s_1\)
- \(s_{gen,cc}=s_3-s_2-\dfrac{q_{combustion}}{T_{source}}\)
- \(s_{gen,gas\ turb}=s_4-s_3\)
- \(s_{gen,HRSG}=(s_5-s_4)+\dfrac{\dot m_s}{\dot m_a}(s_8-s_7)\)  on air basis
- \(s_{gen,pump}=s_7-s_6\)
- \(s_{gen,steam\ turb}=s_9-s_8\)
- \(s_{gen,cond}=s_6-s_9-\dfrac{h_6-h_9}{T_{sink}}\)

Note: \(s_{gen,comp}\), \(s_{gen,cc}\), \(s_{gen,gas\ turb}\) are per kg of air; \(s_{gen,pump}\), \(s_{gen,steam\ turb}\), \(s_{gen,cond}\) are per kg of steam; \(s_{gen,HRSG}\) and \(s_{gen,total}\) are reported on a per-kg-of-air basis.

Using variable-\(c_p\) ideal-gas air on the gas side, saturated/superheated steam-table interpolation on the steam side, and neglecting pressure losses in the combustor/HRSG:

Key relations used:
- Compressor: \(\eta_c=\dfrac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1}\)
- Gas turbine: \(\eta_{gt}=\dfrac{h_3-h_4}{h_3-h_{4s}}\)
- Pump: \(h_{7s}\approx h_6+v_f(P_7-P_6)\), \(\eta_p=\dfrac{h_{7s}-h_6}{h_7-h_6}\)
- Steam turbine: \(\eta_{st}=\dfrac{h_8-h_9}{h_8-h_{9s}}\)
- HRSG: \(\dot m_a(h_4-h_5)=\dot m_s(h_8-h_7)\)
- Combined efficiency: \(\eta_{combined}=\dfrac{\dot W_{net,combined}}{\dot m_a q_{combustion}}\)

Entropy generation:
- \(s_{gen,comp}=s_2-s_1\)
- \(s_{gen,cc}=s_3-s_2-\dfrac{q_{combustion}}{T_{source}}\)
- \(s_{gen,gas\ turb}=s_4-s_3\)
- \(s_{gen,HRSG}=(s_5-s_4)+\dfrac{\dot m_s}{\dot m_a}(s_8-s_7)\)  on air basis
- \(s_{gen,pump}=s_7-s_6\)
- \(s_{gen,steam\ turb}=s_9-s_8\)
- \(s_{gen,cond}=s_6-s_9-\dfrac{h_6-h_9}{T_{sink}}\)

Note: \(s_{gen,comp}\), \(s_{gen,cc}\), \(s_{gen,gas\ turb}\) are per kg of air; \(s_{gen,pump}\), \(s_{gen,steam\ turb}\), \(s_{gen,cond}\) are per kg of steam; \(s_{gen,HRSG}\) and \(s_{gen,total}\) are reported on a per-kg-of-air basis.

Determine all state enthalpies and entropies for both cycles. Calculate component works, heat transfers, combined efficiency. Find entropy generation in each component (compressor, combustion chamber, gas turbine, HRSG, pump, steam turbine, condenser) and total.

raw_response:
Using variable-\(c_p\) ideal-gas air relations on the gas side and standard steam-table interpolation on the water side, with compressed-liquid pump work approximated by \(v_f\Delta P\):

- Gas compressor isentropic state from  
  \[
  s_2^{\circ}-s_1^{\circ}=R\ln\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right),\qquad P_2/P_1=14
  \]
- Actual compressor/turbine from isentropic efficiencies  
  \[
  \eta_c=\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1},\qquad
  \eta_{gt}=\frac{h_3-h_4}{h_3-h_{4s}}
  \]
- Steam side:
  - \(6\): saturated liquid at \(13\) kPa
  - \(7s\): ideal pump outlet, \(h_{7s}=h_6+v_f(P_7-P_6)\)
  - \(7\): actual pump outlet from \(\eta_p\)
  - \(8\): \(11.6\) MPa, superheated by \(76^\circ\text{C}\)
  - \(9s\): isentropic turbine outlet, \(s_{9s}=s_8\)
  - \(9\): actual turbine outlet from \(\eta_{st}\)
- HRSG balance:
  \[
  \dot m_a(h_4-h_5)=\dot m_s(h_8-h_7)
  \]
- Combined power:
  \[
  \dot W_{net}=\dot m_a(w_{gt}-w_c)+\dot m_s(w_{st}-w_p)
  \]
- Combined efficiency:
  \[
  \eta_{combined}=\frac{\dot W_{net}}{\dot m_a q_{comb}}
  \]

For entropy generation:
- Compressor, gas turbine, pump, steam turbine: adiabatic \(\Rightarrow s_{gen}=s_{out}-s_{in}\)
- Combustor:
  \[
  s_{gen,cc}=s_3-s_2-\frac{q_{comb}}{T_{source}}
  \]
- HRSG:
  \[
  s_{gen,HRSG}=(s_5-s_4)+\frac{\dot m_s}{\dot m_a}(s_8-s_7)
  \]
- Condenser:
  \[
  s_{gen,cond}=(s_6-s_9)+\frac{h_9-h_6}{T_{sink}}
  \]

Note: to make the entropy-generation terms additive, the final \(s_{gen}\) values below are reported on a **kg-air basis**.  
(Equivalent steam-basis values are approximately: pump \(0.008\), steam turbine \(0.476\), condenser \(0.480\) kJ/(kg\(_{steam}\)\(\cdot\)K).)

raw_response:
Using variable‑\(c_p\) air on the gas side and interpolated steam‑table properties on the water side:

- Gas side:
  - \(P_2=P_3=r_pP_1=1212\ \text{kPa}\), \(P_4=P_5=P_1=101\ \text{kPa}\)
  - Isentropic states from  
    \[
    \int_{T_1}^{T_{2s}}\frac{c_p(T)}{T}\,dT=R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right),\qquad
    \int_{T_3}^{T_{4s}}\frac{c_p(T)}{T}\,dT=R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_4}{P_3}\right)
    \]
  - Compressor/turbine efficiencies:
    \[
    \eta_c=\frac{h_{2s}-h_1}{h_2-h_1},\qquad
    \eta_{gt}=\frac{h_3-h_4}{h_3-h_{4s}}
    \]

- Steam side:
  - State 6: saturated liquid at \(13\ \text{kPa}\)
  - Pump:
    \[
    h_{7s}=h_6+v_6(P_7-P_6),\qquad
    \eta_p=\frac{h_{7s}-h_6}{h_7-h_6}
    \]
  - State 8: \(11.2\ \text{MPa}\), \(T_8=T_{sat}(11.2\text{ MPa})+43^\circ\text{C}\)
  - Steam turbine:
    \[
    s_{9s}=s_8,\qquad
    \eta_{st}=\frac{h_8-h_9}{h_8-h_{9s}}
    \]

- HRSG balance:
  \[
  \dot m_a(h_4-h_5)=\dot m_s(h_8-h_7)
  \]

- Flow exergy:
  \[
  e_f=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
  \]

- For entropy generation/exergy destruction, I used a plantwide per‑kg‑air basis so the component values sum to the total. Steam-side component values were multiplied by \(\dot m_s/\dot m_a\).

    \]

- HRSG balance:
  \[
  \dot m_a(h_4-h_5)=\dot m_s(h_8-h_7)
  \]

- Flow exergy:
  \[
  e_f=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
  \]
